1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم والنجم إذا هوى
1.english: By the star when it descends,
Your companion [Muhammad] has not strayed, nor has he erred,
1.transliteration: Maa dalla saahibukum wa maa ghawaa

1.english: Nor does he speak from [his own] inclination.
It is not but a revelation revealed,
Taught to him by one intense in strength -
One of soundness. And he rose to [his] true form
While he was in the higher [part of the] horizon.
Then he approached and descended
And was at a distance of two bow lengths or nearer.
And he revealed to His Servant what he revealed.
The heart did not lie [about] what it saw.
1.transliteration: Maa kazabal fu'aadu maa ra aa

1.english: So will you dispute with him over what he saw?
And he certainly saw him in another descent
1.transliteration: Wa laqad ra aahu nazlatan ukhraa

1.english: At the Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary -
Near it is the Garden of Refuge -
When there covered the Lote Tree that which covered [it].
The sight [of the Prophet] did not swerve, nor did it transgress [its limit].
1.transliteration: Maa zaaghal basaru wa maa taghaa

1.arabic: لقد رأى من آيات ربه الكبرى
1.english: He certainly saw of the greatest signs of his Lord.
1.transliteration: Laqad ra aa min aayaati Rabbihil kubraaa

1.english: So have you considered al-Lat and al-'Uzza?
And Manat, the third - the other one?
Is the male for you and for Him the female?
That, then, is an unjust division.

1.arabic: إن هي إلا أسماء سميتموها أنتم وآباؤكم ما أنزل الله بها من سلطان ۚ إن يتبعون إلا الظن وما تهوى الأنفس ۖ ولقد جاءهم من ربهم الهدى
1.english: They are not but [mere] names you have named them - you and your forefathers - for which Allah has sent down no authority. They follow not except assumption and what [their] souls desire, and there has already come to them from their Lord guidance.
1.transliteration: In hiya illaaa asmaaa'un sammaitumoohaaa antum wa aabaaa'ukum maaa anzalal laahu bihaa min sultaan; inyyattabi'oona illaz zanna wa maa tahwal anfusu wa laqad jaaa'ahum mir Rabbihimul hudaa

1.english: Or is there for man whatever he wishes?
1.transliteration: Am lil insaani maa taman naa

1.english: Rather, to Allah belongs the Hereafter and the first [life].

1.arabic: ۞ وكم من ملك في السماوات لا تغني شفاعتهم شيئا إلا من بعد أن يأذن الله لمن يشاء ويرضى
1.english: And how many angels there are in the heavens whose intercession will not avail at all except [only] after Allah has permitted [it] to whom He wills and approves.
1.transliteration: Wa kam mim malakin fissamaawaati laa tughnee shafaa'atuhum shai'an illaa mim ba'di anyyaazanal laahu limany yashaaa'u wa yardaa

1.arabic: إن الذين لا يؤمنون بالآخرة ليسمون الملائكة تسمية الأنثى
1.english: Indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter name the angels female names,
1.transliteration: innal lazeena laa yu'minoona bil aakhirati la yusammoonal malaaa'ikata tasmiyatal unsaa

1.arabic: وما لهم به من علم ۖ إن يتبعون إلا الظن ۖ وإن الظن لا يغني من الحق شيئا
1.english: And they have thereof no knowledge. They follow not except assumption, and indeed, assumption avails not against the truth at all.
1.transliteration: Wa maa lahum bihee min 'ilmin iny yattabi'oona illaz zanna wa innaz zanna laa yughnee minal haqqi shai'aa

1.arabic: فأعرض عن من تولى عن ذكرنا ولم يرد إلا الحياة الدنيا
1.english: So turn away from whoever turns his back on Our message and desires not except the worldly life.
1.transliteration: Fa a'rid 'am man tawallaa 'an zikrinaa wa lam yurid illal hayaatad dunyaa

1.arabic: ذلك مبلغهم من العلم ۚ إن ربك هو أعلم بمن ضل عن سبيله وهو أعلم بمن اهتدى
1.english: That is their sum of knowledge. Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who strays from His way, and He is most knowing of who is guided.
1.transliteration: Zalika mablaghuhum minal 'ilm; inna rabbaka huwa a'lamu biman dalla 'an sabee lihee wa huwa a'lamu bimanih tadaa

1.arabic: ولله ما في السماوات وما في الأرض ليجزي الذين أساءوا بما عملوا ويجزي الذين أحسنوا بالحسنى
1.english: And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth - that He may recompense those who do evil with [the penalty of] what they have done and recompense those who do good with the best [reward] -
1.transliteration: Wa lillaahi maa fis samaawaati wa maa fil ardi liyajziyal lazeena asaaa'oo bimaa 'amiloo wa yajziyal lazeena ahsanoo bilhusnaa

1.arabic: الذين يجتنبون كبائر الإثم والفواحش إلا اللمم ۚ إن ربك واسع المغفرة ۚ هو أعلم بكم إذ أنشأكم من الأرض وإذ أنتم أجنة في بطون أمهاتكم ۖ فلا تزكوا أنفسكم ۖ هو أعلم بمن اتقى
1.english: Those who avoid the major sins and immoralities, only [committing] slight ones. Indeed, your Lord is vast in forgiveness. He was most knowing of you when He produced you from the earth and when you were fetuses in the wombs of your mothers. So do not claim yourselves to be pure; He is most knowing of who fears Him.
1.transliteration: Allazeena yajtaniboona kabaaa'iral ismi walfawaa hisha illal lamam; inna rabbaka waasi'ul maghfirah; huwa a'lamu bikum iz ansha akum minal ardi wa iz antum ajinnatun fee butooni umma haatikum falaa tuzakkooo anfusakum huwa a'lamu bimanit taqaa

1.english: Have you seen the one who turned away
And gave a little and [then] refrained?
Does he have knowledge of the unseen, so he sees?

1.arabic: أم لم ينبأ بما في صحف موسى
1.english: Or has he not been informed of what was in the scriptures of Moses
1.transliteration: Am lam yunabbaa bimaa fee suhuhfi Moosa

1.english: And [of] Abraham, who fulfilled [his obligations] -
That no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another

1.arabic: وأن ليس للإنسان إلا ما سعى
1.english: And that there is not for man except that [good] for which he strives
1.transliteration: Wa al laisa lil insaani illaa maa sa'aa

1.english: And that his effort is going to be seen -
Then he will be recompensed for it with the fullest recompense
And that to your Lord is the finality
And that it is He who makes [one] laugh and weep
1.transliteration: Wa annahoo huwa adhaka wa abkaa

1.english: And that it is He who causes death and gives life
1.transliteration: Wa annahoo huwa amaata wa ahyaa

1.english: And that He creates the two mates - the male and female -
1.transliteration: Wa annahoo khalaqaz zawjainiz zakara wal unsaa

1.english: From a sperm-drop when it is emitted
And that [incumbent] upon Him is the next creation
And that it is He who enriches and suffices
1.transliteration: Wa annahoo huwa aghnaa wa aqnaa

1.english: And that it is He who is the Lord of Sirius
And that He destroyed the first [people of] 'Aad
And Thamud - and He did not spare [them] -

1.arabic: وقوم نوح من قبل ۖ إنهم كانوا هم أظلم وأطغى
1.english: And the people of Noah before. Indeed, it was they who were [even] more unjust and oppressing.
1.transliteration: Wa qawma Noohim min qablu innahum kaanoo hum azlama wa atghaa

1.english: And the overturned towns He hurled down
And covered them by that which He covered.
Then which of the favors of your Lord do you doubt?
This [Prophet] is a warner like the former warners.
The Approaching Day has approached.

1.arabic: ليس لها من دون الله كاشفة
1.english: Of it, [from those] besides Allah, there is no remover.
1.transliteration: Laisa lahaa min doonil laahi kaashifah

1.english: Then at this statement do you wonder?
And you laugh and do not weep
While you are proudly sporting?
So prostrate to Allah and worship [Him].
